description: Byte-locks the parser-differential conformance test per ADR-0037 §05.9.1.
notes: The `cbor_hex` field is the raw envelope bytes (fields 1-8 only; sender_sig_brc31 omitted for byte-equivalence focus).
ACCEPTED vector round-trips byte-identically through canonical encoder.
REJECTED vectors fail re-encode equivalence OR fail strict-parser admission BEFORE re-encode.
Hand-authored REJECTED vectors marked TBD will be byte-locked in the second loop-3 sub-pass.
Recipient action on each vector MUST match `expected_recipient_action`.
Implementations MUST emit `EnvelopeReencodeMismatch` audit event on REJECTED outcomes per ADR-0037.

vectors_accepted.name: accepted-canonical-envelope
vectors_accepted.description: A canonical-CBOR envelope; recipient MUST accept after byte-equivalent re-encode check.
vectors_accepted.re_encode_check: PASS (cbor_hex round-trips to itself under canonical encoding)

vectors_rejected.name: non-minimal-integer-version
vectors_rejected.description: Version field encoded with explicit length byte (0x18 0x01) instead of minimal (0x01). Parses to same value; canonical re-encode produces different bytes.
vectors_rejected.re_encode_check: FAIL — re-encoded canonical bytes differ at offset 2

vectors_rejected.name: indefinite-length-bstr-inner
vectors_rejected.description: Field 8 (inner) encoded as indefinite-length byte string (0x5f...0xff). Forbidden by §05.2.
vectors_rejected.construction_note: Replaced canonical def-length bstr 48|01020304050607080 with indef-length pair 5f|44|01020304|44|05060708|ff.
vectors_rejected.re_encode_check: REJECT before re-encode (CBOR strict-mode decoder MUST refuse indef-length per spec §05.2)

vectors_rejected.name: duplicate-map-key
vectors_rejected.description: Two entries with key 1 (version). Forbidden by canonical CBOR. Map header bumped to 9; second `01 02` inserted after first key-value pair.
vectors_rejected.construction_note: Map-len byte changed a8→a9; inserted extra (01, 02) pair at offset 3.
vectors_rejected.re_encode_check: REJECT during canonical re-encode (canonical CBOR enforces unique keys)

vectors_rejected.name: trailing-bytes
vectors_rejected.description: Canonical envelope followed by trailing 0xFF byte. Forbidden by §05.9.1 #4.
vectors_rejected.re_encode_check: REJECT — input length exceeds parsed length

vectors_rejected.name: float-in-round-field
vectors_rejected.description: Field 5 (round) encoded as float16(1.0) instead of unsigned int 1. Forbidden by §05.2.
vectors_rejected.construction_note: Replaced canonical (05, 01) with (05, f9, 3c, 00) — float16 encoding of 1.0.

vectors_rejected.name: unsorted-map-keys
vectors_rejected.description: Map keys in order 2,1,3..8 instead of canonical 1,2,3..8. Canonical CBOR §4.2 mandates ascending key order.
vectors_rejected.construction_note: Swapped first two key-value pairs (key=1 and key=2) in the canonical envelope.
vectors_rejected.re_encode_check: FAIL — re-encoded canonical-order bytes differ at offset 1

vectors_rejected.name: unknown-tag-on-session-id
vectors_rejected.description: Field 2 (session_id bstr32) wrapped in CBOR tag 0xc0 (datetime). Tag not whitelisted in §05.10.
vectors_rejected.construction_note: Inserted tag byte 0xc0 between key 0x02 and value-header 0x58.
vectors_rejected.re_encode_check: REJECT — tag not whitelisted in §05.10 reserved fields

vectors_rejected.name: bstr-map-key
vectors_rejected.description: Map uses bstr key (0x41 0xff) instead of unsigned integer. §05.3 requires keys be uint.
vectors_rejected.re_encode_check: REJECT — key type not allowed per §05.3 schema
vectors_rejected.violated_rule: §05.9.1 #8 Map keys not uint or tstr
